file.content:
"""Authentication module for Boss直聘 login.

In CDP mode, the user logs in manually in their own Chrome before running
CLI commands. This module verifies login status and provides guidance
if the user is not logged in.
"""

import asyncio
from typing import Optional

from patchright.async_api import Page
from loguru import logger
from rich.console import Console
from rich.panel import Panel

from src.core.browser import BrowserManager, get_browser_manager
from src.utils.captcha import check_captcha
from src.exceptions.errors import SessionExpiredError

console = Console()


async def login(browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None) -> bool:
    """
    Verify login status in CDP-connected Chrome.

    In CDP mode, the user is expected to already be logged in to Boss直聘.
    If not, instructions are printed for manual login in the browser.

    Args:
        browser: Browser manager instance (uses global if None)

    Returns:
        True if logged in
    """
    if browser is None:
        browser = get_browser_manager()

    # Connect to Chrome via CDP
    page = await browser.start()

    # Check if already logged in
    if await check_login_status(page):
        console.print("[green]Already logged in to Boss直聘![/green]")
        # Save session as backup
        await browser.save_session()
        return True

    # Not logged in — guide the user
    console.print(Panel(
        "[bold]Not Logged In[/bold]\n\n"
        "Please log in to Boss直聘 in your Chrome browser:\n"
        "1. Go to https://www.zhipin.com/web/user/?ka=header-login in Chrome\n"
        "2. Scan QR code with Boss直聘 app, OR\n"
        "3. Enter phone number and verification code\n\n"
        "[dim]Then wait here, login will be detected automatically.[/dim]",
        title="Boss直聘 Login",
        border_style="cyan"
    ))

    # Poll for login (user may log in while we wait)
    max_wait_seconds = 300  # 5 minutes timeout
    poll_interval = 3
    elapsed = 0

    console.print("[dim]Waiting for login... (timeout: 5 minutes)[/dim]")

    while elapsed < max_wait_seconds:
        await asyncio.sleep(poll_interval)
        elapsed += poll_interval

        # Check for captcha
        await check_captcha(page)

        if await check_login_status(page):
            console.print("[green]Login successful![/green]")
            await browser.save_session()
            console.print("[green]Session saved as backup[/green]")
            return True

    console.print("[red]Login timeout. Please log in manually and try again.[/red]")
    return False


async def check_login_status(page: Page) -> bool:
    """
    Check if user is currently logged in to Boss直聘 by navigating to a
    protected page and observing whether Boss直聘 redirects to the login page.

    Args:
        page: Playwright page instance

    Returns:
        True if logged in (no redirect to login page)
    """
    try:
        # Navigate to a protected page that requires authentication.
        # The Boss search page reliably triggers server-side auth checks.
        protected_url = "https://www.zhipin.com/web/chat/search"
        await page.goto(protected_url, wait_until='domcontentloaded')

        # Wait for any redirects to settle
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000)

        # Check 1: URL-based detection - did we get redirected to login?
        final_url = page.url
        if 'login' in final_url.lower() or 'signin' in final_url.lower():
            logger.info(f"Login status check: logged_in=False, detail=redirected_to_login (url={final_url})")
            return False

        # Check 2: Page content detection - look for login form elements
        is_login_page = await page.evaluate("""
            () => {
                const bodyText = document.body ? document.body.innerText : '';
                const loginIndicators = [
                    '欢迎登录',
                    '扫码登录',
                    '密码登录',
                    '短信登录',
                    '请输入手机号',
                    '获取验证码',
                    '微信登录',
                    '登录/注册'
                ];
                for (const indicator of loginIndicators) {
                    if (bodyText.includes(indicator)) {
                        return { is_login: true, matched: indicator };
                    }
                }

                // Also check for login form elements in DOM
                const loginForm = document.querySelector(
                    'input[type="tel"], input[placeholder*="手机"], ' +
                    '[class*="qrcode"], [class*="scan-login"], ' +
                    '[class*="login-form"], [class*="sign-form"]'
                );
                if (loginForm) {
                    return { is_login: true, matched: 'login_form_element' };
                }

                return { is_login: false, matched: null };
            }
        """)

        if is_login_page.get('is_login', False):
            detail = is_login_page.get('matched', 'unknown')
            logger.info(f"Login status check: logged_in=False, detail=login_page_detected ({detail})")
            return False

        logger.info(f"Login status check: logged_in=True, detail=protected_page_accessible (url={final_url})")
        return True

    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Error checking login status: {e}")
        return False


async def ensure_logged_in(browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None) -> bool:
    """
    Ensure user is logged in, prompting for login if needed.

    Args:
        browser: Browser manager instance

    Returns:
        True if logged in (existing or new login)

    Raises:
        SessionExpiredError: If login fails
    """
    if browser is None:
        browser = get_browser_manager()

    # Connect to Chrome via CDP if not already connected
    if not browser.is_running:
        await browser.start()

    # Check login status
    if browser.page:
        if await check_login_status(browser.page):
            return True

    # Not logged in — guide user
    console.print("[yellow]Not logged in. Please log in to Boss直聘 in your Chrome browser.[/yellow]")

    if await login(browser):
        return True

    raise SessionExpiredError("Not logged in to Boss直聘. Please log in manually in Chrome and try again.")

1→"""Profile extraction module for Boss直聘.
     2→
     3→Extracts detailed candidate profile information from Boss直聘.
     4→Profile details are shown as a floating overlay (浮层) on the search page,
     5→not as a separate page. The extraction JS also supports standalone profile
     6→pages as a fallback.
     7→"""
     8→
     9→from typing import Optional, Dict, Any
    10→
    11→from patchright.async_api import Page
    12→from loguru import logger
    13→from rich.console import Console
    14→from rich.panel import Panel
    15→
    16→from src.core.browser import BrowserManager, get_browser_manager
    17→from src.utils.delays import async_random_delay
    18→from src.utils.rate_limiter import get_rate_limiter, async_check_rate_limit
    19→from src.utils.captcha import check_captcha
    20→from src.models.database import Candidate, get_db_manager
    21→from src.exceptions.errors import ExtractionError
    22→
    23→console = Console()
    24→
    25→
    26→# JavaScript extraction function for Boss直聘 profile pages
    27→EXTRACT_PROFILE_JS = """
    28→() => {
    29→    const result = {
    30→        name: '',
    31→        title: '',
    32→        company: '',
    33→        location: '',
    34→        salary_expect: '',
    35→        work_years: '',
    36→        education_level: '',
    37→        basic_info: {},
    38→        education: [],
    39→        work_experience: [],
    40→        skills: []
    41→    };
    42→
    43→    // Extract basic info from the profile header area
    44→    const nameEl = document.querySelector(
    45→        '[class*="geek-name"], [class*="name"], h1[class*="name"], .name'
    46→    );
    47→    result.name = nameEl ? nameEl.innerText.trim() : '';
    48→
    49→    const titleEl = document.querySelector(
    50→        '[class*="expect-position"], [class*="title"], [class*="position"]'
    51→    );
    52→    result.title = titleEl ? titleEl.innerText.trim() : '';
    53→
    54→    const companyEl = document.querySelector(
    55→        '[class*="company-name"], [class*="company"]'
    56→    );
    57→    result.company = companyEl ? companyEl.innerText.trim() : '';
    58→
    59→    const locationEl = document.querySelector(
    60→        '[class*="expect-area"], [class*="location"], [class*="city"]'
    61→    );
    62→    result.location = locationEl ? locationEl.innerText.trim() : '';
    63→
    64→    const salaryEl = document.querySelector(
    65→        '[class*="expect-salary"], [class*="salary"]'
    66→    );
    67→    result.salary_expect = salaryEl ? salaryEl.innerText.trim() : '';
    68→
    69→    // Extract info tags (work years, education, age, etc.)
    70→    const tagEls = document.querySelectorAll(
    71→        '[class*="info-tag"], [class*="base-info"] span, [class*="geek-tags"] span, ' +
    72→        '[class*="tag-item"], [class*="basic-info"] span'
    73→    );
    74→    const tags = Array.from(tagEls).map(el => el.innerText.trim()).filter(t => t);
    75→
    76→    for (const tag of tags) {
    77→        if (tag.includes('年') && (tag.includes('经验') || /^\\d/.test(tag))) {
    78→            result.work_years = tag;
    79→            result.basic_info.work_years = tag;
    80→        } else if (['博士', '硕士', '本科', '大专', '中专', '高中'].some(d => tag.includes(d))) {
    81→            result.education_level = tag;
    82→            result.basic_info.education = tag;
    83→        } else if (tag.includes('岁')) {
    84→            result.basic_info.age = tag;
    85→        } else if (tag.includes('求职') || tag.includes('状态')) {
    86→            result.basic_info.job_status = tag;
    87→        }
    88→    }
    89→
    90→    // Extract education details
    91→    const eduSection = document.querySelector(
    92→        '[class*="resume-education"], [class*="education-list"], [class*="edu-experience"]'
    93→    );
    94→    if (eduSection) {
    95→        const eduItems = eduSection.querySelectorAll(
    96→            '[class*="resume-item"], [class*="edu-item"], [class*="item"], li'
    97→        );
    98→        for (const item of eduItems) {
    99→            const text = item.innerText.trim();
   100→            if (!text) continue;
   101→            const lines = text.split('\\n').filter(l => l.trim());
   102→            const edu = {};
   103→            if (lines.length >= 1) edu.school = lines[0].trim();
   104→            if (lines.length >= 2) edu.degree = lines[1].trim();
   105→            if (lines.length >= 3) edu.major = lines[2].trim();
   106→            if (lines.length >= 4) edu.period = lines[3].trim();
   107→            result.education.push(edu);
   108→        }
   109→    }
   110→
   111→    // Extract work experience
   112→    const workSection = document.querySelector(
   113→        '[class*="resume-work"], [class*="work-list"], [class*="work-experience"]'
   114→    );
   115→    if (workSection) {
   116→        const workItems = workSection.querySelectorAll(
   117→            '[class*="resume-item"], [class*="work-item"], [class*="item"], li'
   118→        );
   119→        for (const item of workItems) {
   120→            const text = item.innerText.trim();
   121→            if (!text) continue;
   122→            const lines = text.split('\\n').filter(l => l.trim());
   123→            const work = {};
   124→            if (lines.length >= 1) work.company = lines[0].trim();
   125→            if (lines.length >= 2) work.title = lines[1].trim();
   126→            if (lines.length >= 3) work.period = lines[2].trim();
   127→            if (lines.length >= 4) work.description = lines.slice(3).join(' ').trim();
   128→            result.work_experience.push(work);
   129→        }
   130→    }
   131→
   132→    // Extract project experience (Boss直聘 specific)
   133→    const projectSection = document.querySelector(
   134→        '[class*="resume-project"], [class*="project-list"]'
   135→    );
   136→    if (projectSection) {
   137→        result.basic_info.has_project = true;
   138→    }
   139→
   140→    // Extract skills
   141→    const skillEls = document.querySelectorAll(
   142→        '[class*="skill-tag"], [class*="skill-item"], [class*="tag-item"], ' +
   143→        '[class*="skill"] span'
   144→    );
   145→    const skills = new Set();
   146→    for (const el of skillEls) {
   147→        const text = el.innerText.trim();
   148→        if (text && text.length < 30) {
   149→            skills.add(text);
   150→        }
   151→    }
   152→    result.skills = Array.from(skills);
   153→
   154→    // Extract advantages/self description
   155→    const advantageEl = document.querySelector(
   156→        '[class*="resume-advantage"], [class*="self-desc"], [class*="geek-advantage"]'
   157→    );
   158→    if (advantageEl) {
   159→        result.basic_info.self_description = advantageEl.innerText.trim();
   160→    }
   161→
   162→    return result;
   163→}
   164→"""
   165→
   166→
   167→async def extract_profile(
   168→    boss_id: str,
   169→    browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None,
   170→) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   171→    """
   172→    Extract detailed profile for a candidate.
   173→
   174→    Args:
   175→        boss_id: Boss直聘 candidate ID (e.g., "zp_xxx")
   176→        browser: Browser manager instance
   177→
   178→    Returns:
   179→        Dictionary with profile data
   180→
   181→    Raises:
   182→        ExtractionError: If profile extraction fails
   183→    """
   184→    if browser is None:
   185→        browser = get_browser_manager()
   186→
   187→    page = browser.page
   188→    if not page:
   189→        page = await browser.start()
   190→
   191→    rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()
   192→    db_manager = get_db_manager()
   193→
   194→    # Rate limit check
   195→    await async_check_rate_limit('view_profile')
   196→
   197→    # Boss直聘 shows profile as a floating overlay (浮层) on the search page.
   198→    # Strategy 1: Try to extract from an already-open overlay on the current page.
   199→    # Strategy 2: Navigate to profile URL as a fallback.
   200→    encrypt_id = boss_id.replace('zp_', '') if boss_id.startswith('zp_') else boss_id
   201→
   202→    # First check if we have a profile URL in the database
   203→    profile_url = None
   204→    with db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   205→        candidate = session.query(Candidate).filter_by(boss_id=boss_id).first()
   206→        if candidate and candidate.profile_url:
   207→            profile_url = candidate.profile_url
   208→
   209→    if not profile_url:
   210→        profile_url = f"https://www.zhipin.com/web/geek/{encrypt_id}"
   211→
   212→    try:
   213→        # Strategy 1: Check if a profile overlay is already open on the current page
   214→        overlay_present = await page.evaluate("""
   215→            () => {
   216→                const overlay = document.querySelector(
   217→                    '[class*="dialog-wrap"].active, [class*="boss-popup__wrapper"],
   218→                     [class*="geek-detail"], [class*="resume-dialog"], [class*="profile-panel"]'
   219→                );
   220→                return overlay !== null;
   221→            }
   222→        """)
   223→
   224→        if not overlay_present:
   225→            # Strategy 2: Navigate to profile page
   226→            await page.goto(profile_url, wait_until='domcontentloaded')
   227→            await async_random_delay('page_load')
   228→
   229→            # Check for captcha
   230→            await check_captcha(page, report_to_rate_limiter=True)
   231→
   232→            # Wait for profile content to load
   233→            await page.wait_for_timeout(2000)
   234→
   235→        # Extract profile data via JavaScript (works on both overlay and standalone page)
   236→        profile_data = await page.evaluate(EXTRACT_PROFILE_JS)
   237→
   238→        if not profile_data or not profile_data.get('name'):
   239→            logger.warning(f"Could not extract profile for {boss_id}")
   240→            raise ExtractionError(f"Failed to extract profile data for {boss_id}")
   241→
   242→        # Log action
   243→        rate_limiter.log_action('view_profile', {'boss_id': boss_id})
   244→
   245→        # Update database
   246→        _update_profile_in_db(boss_id, profile_data, db_manager)
   247→
   248→        # Display profile
   249→        _display_profile(boss_id, profile_data)
   250→
   251→        return profile_data
   252→
   253→    except ExtractionError:
   254→        raise
   255→    except Exception as e:
   256→        logger.error(f"Error extracting profile for {boss_id}: {e}")
   257→        raise ExtractionError(f"Failed to extract profile for {boss_id}: {e}")
   258→
   259→
   260→def _update_profile_in_db(boss_id: str, profile_data: Dict, db_manager) -> None:
   261→    """Update candidate profile in the database."""
   262→    with db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   263→        candidate = session.query(Candidate).filter_by(boss_id=boss_id).first()
   264→
   265→        if not candidate:
   266→            # Create new candidate entry
   267→            candidate = Candidate(boss_id=boss_id)
   268→            session.add(candidate)
   269→
   270→        # Update fields
   271→        candidate.name = profile_data.get('name') or candidate.name
   272→        candidate.title = profile_data.get('title') or candidate.title
   273→        candidate.company = profile_data.get('company') or candidate.company
   274→        candidate.location = profile_data.get('location') or candidate.location
   275→        candidate.salary_expect = profile_data.get('salary_expect') or candidate.salary_expect
   276→        candidate.work_years = profile_data.get('work_years') or candidate.work_years
   277→        candidate.education_level = profile_data.get('education_level') or candidate.education_level
   278→        candidate.basic_info = profile_data.get('basic_info')
   279→        candidate.education = profile_data.get('education')
   280→        candidate.work_experience = profile_data.get('work_experience')
   281→        candidate.skills = profile_data.get('skills')
   282→
   283→
   284→def _display_profile(boss_id: str, profile_data: Dict) -> None:
   285→    """Display extracted profile in Rich format."""
   286→    name = profile_data.get('name', 'Unknown')
   287→    title = profile_data.get('title', '')
   288→    company = profile_data.get('company', '')
   289→    location = profile_data.get('location', '')
   290→    salary = profile_data.get('salary_expect', '')
   291→    work_years = profile_data.get('work_years', '')
   292→    education_level = profile_data.get('education_level', '')
   293→
   294→    # Header
   295→    header = f"[bold]{name}[/bold]"
   296→    if title:
   297→        header += f" - {title}"
   298→    if company:
   299→        header += f" @ {company}"
   300→
   301→    console.print(Panel(header, title=f"Profile: {boss_id}", border_style="green"))
   302→
   303→    # Basic info
   304→    info_parts = []
   305→    if location:
   306→        info_parts.append(f"Location: {location}")
   307→    if salary:
   308→        info_parts.append(f"Expected Salary: {salary}")
   309→    if work_years:
   310→        info_parts.append(f"Experience: {work_years}")
   311→    if education_level:
   312→        info_parts.append(f"Education: {education_level}")
   313→
   314→    if info_parts:
   315→        console.print("  " + " | ".join(info_parts))
   316→
   317→    # Education
   318→    education = profile_data.get('education', [])
   319→    if education:
   320→        console.print("\n  [cyan]Education:[/cyan]")
   321→        for edu in education:
   322→            parts = []
   323→            if edu.get('school'):
   324→                parts.append(edu['school'])
   325→            if edu.get('degree'):
   326→                parts.append(edu['degree'])
   327→            if edu.get('major'):
   328→                parts.append(edu['major'])
   329→            console.print(f"    - {' | '.join(parts)}")
   330→
   331→    # Work experience
   332→    work_exp = profile_data.get('work_experience', [])
   333→    if work_exp:
   334→        console.print("\n  [cyan]Work Experience:[/cyan]")
   335→        for work in work_exp:
   336→            line = ""
   337→            if work.get('company'):
   338→                line += work['company']
   339→            if work.get('title'):
   340→                line += f" - {work['title']}"
   341→            if work.get('period'):
   342→                line += f" ({work['period']})"
   343→            console.print(f"    - {line}")
   344→
   345→    # Skills
   346→    skills = profile_data.get('skills', [])
   347→    if skills:
   348→        console.print(f"\n  [cyan]Skills:[/cyan] {', '.join(skills)}")
   349→
   350→    # Self description
   351→    basic_info = profile_data.get('basic_info', {})
   352→    if basic_info.get('self_description'):
   353→        desc = basic_info['self_description'][:200]
   354→        console.print(f"\n  [cyan]Self Description:[/cyan]\n    {desc}")
   355→
   356→    console.print()
   357→

1→"""Rate limiter for preventing account bans."""
     2→
     3→import asyncio
     4→from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
     5→from typing import Tuple, Optional
     6→
     7→from loguru import logger
     8→from sqlalchemy import func
     9→
    10→from src.models.database import ActionLog, get_db_manager, DatabaseManager
    11→from src.utils.config import get_config, Config
    12→from src.exceptions.errors import RateLimitExceededError
    13→
    14→
    15→class RateLimiter:
    16→    """Rate limiter for controlling action frequency.
    17→
    18→    Supports adaptive throttling: when captchas or errors are reported,
    19→    limits are temporarily tightened to reduce detection risk.
    20→    """
    21→
    22→    def __init__(self, config: Optional[Config] = None, db_manager: Optional[DatabaseManager] = None):
    23→        self.config = config or get_config()
    24→        self.db_manager = db_manager or get_db_manager()
    25→
    26→        self._throttle_factor: float = 1.0
    27→        self._captcha_timestamps: list = []
    28→        self._error_timestamps: list = []
    29→        self._pruned: bool = False
    30→
    31→    def report_captcha(self) -> None:
    32→        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    33→        self._captcha_timestamps.append(now)
    34→        cutoff = now - timedelta(minutes=30)
    35→        self._captcha_timestamps = [t for t in self._captcha_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
    36→        self._update_throttle_factor()
    37→
    38→    def report_error(self) -> None:
    39→        """Report an error to adaptive throttling (e.g. page load failure, selector not found)."""
    40→        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    41→        self._error_timestamps.append(now)
    42→        cutoff = now - timedelta(minutes=30)
    43→        self._error_timestamps = [t for t in self._error_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
    44→        self._update_throttle_factor()
    45→
    46→    def _update_throttle_factor(self) -> None:
    47→        captcha_count = len(self._captcha_timestamps)
    48→        error_count = len(self._error_timestamps)
    49→        combined = captcha_count + error_count * 0.5
    50→
    51→        if combined >= 3:
    52→            self._throttle_factor = 0.3
    53→        elif combined >= 2:
    54→            self._throttle_factor = 0.5
    55→        elif combined >= 1:
    56→            self._throttle_factor = 0.7
    57→        else:
    58→            self._throttle_factor = 1.0
    59→
    60→        if self._throttle_factor < 1.0:
    61→            logger.warning(
    62→                f"Adaptive throttle active: factor={self._throttle_factor:.1f} "
    63→                f"(captchas={captcha_count}, errors={error_count} in last 30min)"
    64→            )
    65→
    66→    def _refresh_throttle(self) -> None:
    67→        cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(minutes=30)
    68→        self._captcha_timestamps = [t for t in self._captcha_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
    69→        self._error_timestamps = [t for t in self._error_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
    70→        self._update_throttle_factor()
    71→
    72→    @property
    73→    def throttle_factor(self) -> float:
    74→        return self._throttle_factor
    75→
    76→    def _get_limits(self, action_type: str) -> Tuple[int, int, int]:
    77→        action_map = {
    78→            'search': ('search_per_hour', 'search_per_day'),
    79→            'view_profile': ('profile_view_per_hour', 'profile_view_per_day'),
    80→            'send_message': ('message_per_hour', 'message_per_day'),
    81→        }
    82→
    83→        if action_type not in action_map:
    84→            raise ValueError(f"Unknown action type: {action_type}")
    85→
    86→        hour_key, day_key = action_map[action_type]
    87→
    88→        per_hour = self.config.get(f'rate_limits.{hour_key}', 20)
    89→        per_day = self.config.get(f'rate_limits.{day_key}', 100)
    90→        min_interval = self.config.get('rate_limits.min_interval_seconds', 35)
    91→
    92→        per_hour = max(1, int(per_hour * self._throttle_factor))
    93→        per_day = max(1, int(per_day * self._throttle_factor))
    94→        if self._throttle_factor < 1.0:
    95→            min_interval = int(min_interval / self._throttle_factor)
    96→
    97→        return per_hour, per_day, min_interval
    98→
    99→    def _count_actions(self, action_type: str, since: datetime) -> int:
   100→        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   101→            count = session.query(func.count(ActionLog.id)).filter(
   102→                ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
   103→                ActionLog.timestamp >= since
   104→            ).scalar()
   105→            return count or 0
   106→
   107→    def _get_last_action_time(self, action_type: Optional[str] = None) -> Optional[datetime]:
   108→        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   109→            query = session.query(func.max(ActionLog.timestamp))
   110→            if action_type:
   111→                query = query.filter(ActionLog.action_type == action_type)
   112→            last_time = query.scalar()
   113→            return last_time
   114→
   115→    def check_rate_limit(self, action_type: str) -> None:
   116→        if not self._pruned:
   117→            self.prune_old_entries()
   118→            self._pruned = True
   119→
   120→        self._refresh_throttle()
   121→        per_hour, per_day, min_interval = self._get_limits(action_type)
   122→
   123→        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
   124→        one_hour_ago = now - timedelta(hours=1)
   125→        one_day_ago = now - timedelta(days=1)
   126→
   127→        hourly_count = self._count_actions(action_type, one_hour_ago)
   128→        if hourly_count >= per_hour:
   129→            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   130→                oldest_in_window = session.query(ActionLog.timestamp).filter(
   131→                    ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
   132→                    ActionLog.timestamp >= one_hour_ago
   133→                ).order_by(ActionLog.timestamp.asc()).first()
   134→
   135→                if oldest_in_window:
   136→                    wait_until = oldest_in_window[0] + timedelta(hours=1)
   137→                    wait_seconds = max(1, int((wait_until - now).total_seconds()))
   138→                else:
   139→                    wait_seconds = 3600
   140→
   141→            raise RateLimitExceededError(
   142→                action_type=action_type,
   143→                wait_time=wait_seconds,
   144→                message=f"Hourly rate limit exceeded for {action_type}. "
   145→                        f"Limit: {per_hour}/hour. Wait {wait_seconds // 60} minutes."
   146→            )
   147→
   148→        daily_count = self._count_actions(action_type, one_day_ago)
   149→        if daily_count >= per_day:
   150→            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   151→                oldest_in_window = session.query(ActionLog.timestamp).filter(
   152→                    ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
   153→                    ActionLog.timestamp >= one_day_ago
   154→                ).order_by(ActionLog.timestamp.asc()).first()
   155→
   156→                if oldest_in_window:
   157→                    wait_until = oldest_in_window[0] + timedelta(days=1)
   158→                    wait_seconds = max(1, int((wait_until - now).total_seconds()))
   159→                else:
   160→                    wait_seconds = 86400
   161→
   162→            raise RateLimitExceededError(
   163→                action_type=action_type,
   164→                wait_time=wait_seconds,
   165→                message=f"Daily rate limit exceeded for {action_type}. "
   166→                        f"Limit: {per_day}/day. Wait {wait_seconds // 3600} hours."
   167→            )
   168→
   169→        last_action = self._get_last_action_time(action_type=action_type)
   170→        if last_action:
   171→            elapsed = (now - last_action).total_seconds()
   172→            if elapsed < min_interval:
   173→                wait_seconds = max(1, int(min_interval - elapsed))
   174→                raise RateLimitExceededError(
   175→                    action_type=action_type,
   176→                    wait_time=wait_seconds,
   177→                    message=f"Minimum interval not met for {action_type}. Wait {wait_seconds} seconds."
   178→                )
   179→
   180→        logger.debug(f"Rate limit check passed for {action_type}")
   181→
   182→    def log_action(self, action_type: str, details: Optional[dict] = None) -> None:
   183→        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   184→            action_log = ActionLog(
   185→                action_type=action_type,
   186→                timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
   187→                details=details or {}
   188→            )
   189→            session.add(action_log)
   190→        logger.debug(f"Logged action: {action_type}")
   191→
   192→    def prune_old_entries(self, retention_hours: int = 48) -> int:
   193→        cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(hours=retention_hours)
   194→        try:
   195→            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   196→                count = session.query(ActionLog).filter(
   197→                    ActionLog.timestamp < cutoff
   198→                ).delete()
   199→                logger.debug(f"Pruned {count} old ActionLog entries (older than {retention_hours}h)")
   200→                return count
   201→        except Exception as e:
   202→            logger.error(f"Failed to prune ActionLog entries: {e}")
   203→            return 0
   204→
   205→    def get_quota_status(self, action_type: str) -> dict:
   206→        per_hour, per_day, _ = self._get_limits(action_type)
   207→
   208→        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
   209→        one_hour_ago = now - timedelta(hours=1)
   210→        one_day_ago = now - timedelta(days=1)
   211→
   212→        hourly_used = self._count_actions(action_type, one_hour_ago)
   213→        daily_used = self._count_actions(action_type, one_day_ago)
   214→
   215→        return {
   216→            'action_type': action_type,
   217→            'hourly': {
   218→                'used': hourly_used,
   219→                'limit': per_hour,
   220→                'remaining': max(0, per_hour - hourly_used)
   221→            },
   222→            'daily': {
   223→                'used': daily_used,
   224→                'limit': per_day,
   225→                'remaining': max(0, per_day - daily_used)
   226→            }
   227→        }
   228→
   229→
   230→async def async_check_rate_limit(action_type: str, max_retries: int = 60, max_wait_per_retry: int = 3600) -> None:
   231→    rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()
   232→    for attempt in range(max_retries):
   233→        try:
   234→            rate_limiter.check_rate_limit(action_type)
   235→            return
   236→        except RateLimitExceededError as e:
   237→            if attempt >= max_retries - 1:
   238→                raise
   239→            wait = min(e.wait_time + 1, max_wait_per_retry)
   240→            logger.info(f"Rate limit hit: {e}. Waiting {wait}s before retrying (attempt {attempt + 1}/{max_retries})...")
   241→            await asyncio.sleep(wait)
   242→
   243→
   244→_rate_limiter = None
   245→
   246→
   247→def get_rate_limiter() -> RateLimiter:
   248→    global _rate_limiter
   249→    if _rate_limiter is None:
   250→        _rate_limiter = RateLimiter()
   251→    return _rate_limiter
   252→
   253→
   254→def reset_rate_limiter() -> None:
   255→    """Reset global rate limiter instance (for testing)."""
   256→    global _rate_limiter
   257→    _rate_limiter = None
   258→

"""Rate limiter for preventing account bans."""

import asyncio
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from typing import Tuple, Optional

from loguru import logger
from sqlalchemy import func

from src.models.database import ActionLog, get_db_manager, DatabaseManager
from src.utils.config import get_config, Config
from src.exceptions.errors import RateLimitExceededError


class RateLimiter:
    """Rate limiter for controlling action frequency.

    Supports adaptive throttling: when captchas or errors are reported,
    limits are temporarily tightened to reduce detection risk.
    """

    def __init__(self, config: Optional[Config] = None, db_manager: Optional[DatabaseManager] = None):
        self.config = config or get_config()
        self.db_manager = db_manager or get_db_manager()

        self._throttle_factor: float = 1.0
        self._captcha_timestamps: list = []
        self._error_timestamps: list = []
        self._pruned: bool = False

    def report_captcha(self) -> None:
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        self._captcha_timestamps.append(now)
        cutoff = now - timedelta(minutes=30)
        self._captcha_timestamps = [t for t in self._captcha_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
        self._update_throttle_factor()

    def report_error(self) -> None:
        """Report an error to adaptive throttling (e.g. page load failure, selector not found)."""
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        self._error_timestamps.append(now)
        cutoff = now - timedelta(minutes=30)
        self._error_timestamps = [t for t in self._error_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
        self._update_throttle_factor()

    def _update_throttle_factor(self) -> None:
        captcha_count = len(self._captcha_timestamps)
        error_count = len(self._error_timestamps)
        combined = captcha_count + error_count * 0.5

        if combined >= 3:
            self._throttle_factor = 0.3
        elif combined >= 2:
            self._throttle_factor = 0.5
        elif combined >= 1:
            self._throttle_factor = 0.7
        else:
            self._throttle_factor = 1.0

        if self._throttle_factor < 1.0:
            logger.warning(
                f"Adaptive throttle active: factor={self._throttle_factor:.1f} "
                f"(captchas={captcha_count}, errors={error_count} in last 30min)"
            )

    def _refresh_throttle(self) -> None:
        cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(minutes=30)
        self._captcha_timestamps = [t for t in self._captcha_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
        self._error_timestamps = [t for t in self._error_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
        self._update_throttle_factor()

    @property
    def throttle_factor(self) -> float:
        return self._throttle_factor

    def _get_limits(self, action_type: str) -> Tuple[int, int, int]:
        action_map = {
            'search': ('search_per_hour', 'search_per_day'),
            'view_profile': ('profile_view_per_hour', 'profile_view_per_day'),
            'send_message': ('message_per_hour', 'message_per_day'),
        }

        if action_type not in action_map:
            raise ValueError(f"Unknown action type: {action_type}")

        hour_key, day_key = action_map[action_type]

        per_hour = self.config.get(f'rate_limits.{hour_key}', 20)
        per_day = self.config.get(f'rate_limits.{day_key}', 100)
        min_interval = self.config.get('rate_limits.min_interval_seconds', 35)

        per_hour = max(1, int(per_hour * self._throttle_factor))
        per_day = max(1, int(per_day * self._throttle_factor))
        if self._throttle_factor < 1.0:
            min_interval = int(min_interval / self._throttle_factor)

        return per_hour, per_day, min_interval

    def _count_actions(self, action_type: str, since: datetime) -> int:
        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
            count = session.query(func.count(ActionLog.id)).filter(
                ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
                ActionLog.timestamp >= since
            ).scalar()
            return count or 0

    def _get_last_action_time(self, action_type: Optional[str] = None) -> Optional[datetime]:
        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
            query = session.query(func.max(ActionLog.timestamp))
            if action_type:
                query = query.filter(ActionLog.action_type == action_type)
            last_time = query.scalar()
            return last_time

    def check_rate_limit(self, action_type: str) -> None:
        if not self._pruned:
            self.prune_old_entries()
            self._pruned = True

        self._refresh_throttle()
        per_hour, per_day, min_interval = self._get_limits(action_type)

        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        one_hour_ago = now - timedelta(hours=1)
        one_day_ago = now - timedelta(days=1)

        hourly_count = self._count_actions(action_type, one_hour_ago)
        if hourly_count >= per_hour:
            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
                oldest_in_window = session.query(ActionLog.timestamp).filter(
                    ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
                    ActionLog.timestamp >= one_hour_ago
                ).order_by(ActionLog.timestamp.asc()).first()

                if oldest_in_window:
                    wait_until = oldest_in_window[0] + timedelta(hours=1)
                    wait_seconds = max(1, int((wait_until - now).total_seconds()))
                else:
                    wait_seconds = 3600

            raise RateLimitExceededError(
                action_type=action_type,
                wait_time=wait_seconds,
                message=f"Hourly rate limit exceeded for {action_type}. "
                        f"Limit: {per_hour}/hour. Wait {wait_seconds // 60} minutes."
            )

        daily_count = self._count_actions(action_type, one_day_ago)
        if daily_count >= per_day:
            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
                oldest_in_window = session.query(ActionLog.timestamp).filter(
                    ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
                    ActionLog.timestamp >= one_day_ago
                ).order_by(ActionLog.timestamp.asc()).first()

                if oldest_in_window:
                    wait_until = oldest_in_window[0] + timedelta(days=1)
                    wait_seconds = max(1, int((wait_until - now).total_seconds()))
                else:
                    wait_seconds = 86400

            raise RateLimitExceededError(
                action_type=action_type,
                wait_time=wait_seconds,
                message=f"Daily rate limit exceeded for {action_type}. "
                        f"Limit: {per_day}/day. Wait {wait_seconds // 3600} hours."
            )

        last_action = self._get_last_action_time(action_type=action_type)
        if last_action:
            elapsed = (now - last_action).total_seconds()
            if elapsed < min_interval:
                wait_seconds = max(1, int(min_interval - elapsed))
                raise RateLimitExceededError(
                    action_type=action_type,
                    wait_time=wait_seconds,
                    message=f"Minimum interval not met for {action_type}. Wait {wait_seconds} seconds."
                )

        logger.debug(f"Rate limit check passed for {action_type}")

    def log_action(self, action_type: str, details: Optional[dict] = None) -> None:
        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
            action_log = ActionLog(
                action_type=action_type,
                timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
                details=details or {}
            )
            session.add(action_log)
        logger.debug(f"Logged action: {action_type}")

    def prune_old_entries(self, retention_hours: int = 48) -> int:
        cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(hours=retention_hours)
        try:
            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
                count = session.query(ActionLog).filter(
                    ActionLog.timestamp < cutoff
                ).delete()
                logger.debug(f"Pruned {count} old ActionLog entries (older than {retention_hours}h)")
                return count
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Failed to prune ActionLog entries: {e}")
            return 0

    def get_quota_status(self, action_type: str) -> dict:
        per_hour, per_day, _ = self._get_limits(action_type)

        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        one_hour_ago = now - timedelta(hours=1)
        one_day_ago = now - timedelta(days=1)

        hourly_used = self._count_actions(action_type, one_hour_ago)
        daily_used = self._count_actions(action_type, one_day_ago)

        return {
            'action_type': action_type,
            'hourly': {
                'used': hourly_used,
                'limit': per_hour,
                'remaining': max(0, per_hour - hourly_used)
            },
            'daily': {
                'used': daily_used,
                'limit': per_day,
                'remaining': max(0, per_day - daily_used)
            }
        }


async def async_check_rate_limit(action_type: str, max_retries: int = 60, max_wait_per_retry: int = 3600) -> None:
    rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()
    for attempt in range(max_retries):
        try:
            rate_limiter.check_rate_limit(action_type)
            return
        except RateLimitExceededError as e:
            if attempt >= max_retries - 1:
                raise
            wait = min(e.wait_time + 1, max_wait_per_retry)
            logger.info(f"Rate limit hit: {e}. Waiting {wait}s before retrying (attempt {attempt + 1}/{max_retries})...")
            await asyncio.sleep(wait)


_rate_limiter = None


def get_rate_limiter() -> RateLimiter:
    global _rate_limiter
    if _rate_limiter is None:
        _rate_limiter = RateLimiter()
    return _rate_limiter


def reset_rate_limiter() -> None:
    """Reset global rate limiter instance (for testing)."""
    global _rate_limiter
    _rate_limiter = None

    12→class Config:
    13→    """Configuration manager for Boss Assistant."""
    14→
    15→    def __init__(self, config_dir: str = None):
    16→        if config_dir is None:
    17→            # Use project root (where this source file lives) to find config/
    18→            config_dir = str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent / "config")
    19→        self.config_dir = Path(config_dir)
    20→        self._settings: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    21→        self._selectors: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    22→        self._message_templates: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    23→
    24→        self._load_all()
    25→
    26→    def _load_json(self, filename: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    27→        filepath = self.config_dir / filename
    28→        try:
    29→            with open(filepath, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    30→                return json.load(f)
    31→        except FileNotFoundError:
    32→            raise ConfigurationError(f"Configuration file not found: {filepath}")
    33→        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
    34→            raise ConfigurationError(f"Invalid JSON in {filepath}: {e}")
    35→
    36→    def _load_all(self):
    37→        logger.info("Loading configuration files...")
    38→        self._settings = self._load_json("settings.json")
    39→        self._selectors = self._load_json("selectors.json")
    40→        self._message_templates = self._load_json("message_templates.json")
    41→        logger.info("Configuration loaded successfully")
    42→
    43→    _MISSING = object()
    44→
    45→    def get(self, key: str, default: Any = None) -> Any:
    46→        """Get setting by dot-notation key.
    47→
    48→        Distinguishes between a key not found and a key explicitly set to null.
    49→        """
    50→        keys = key.split('.')
    51→        value = self._settings
    52→
    53→        for k in keys:
    54→            if isinstance(value, dict):
    55→                value = value.get(k, self._MISSING)
    56→                if value is self._MISSING:
    57→                    return default
    58→            else:
    59→                return default
    60→
    61→        return value
    62→
    63→    def get_selector(self, selector_path: str) -> str:
    64→        """Get selector by dot-notation path."""
    65→        keys = selector_path.split('.')
    66→        value = self._selectors.get('selectors', {})
    67→
    68→        for k in keys:
    69→            if isinstance(value, dict):
    70→                value = value.get(k)
    71→                if value is None:
    72→                    raise ConfigurationError(f"Selector not found: {selector_path}")
    73→            else:
    74→                raise ConfigurationError(f"Invalid selector path: {selector_path}")
    75→
    76→        return value
    77→
    78→    def get_template(self, template_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    79→        template = self._message_templates.get(template_name)
    80→        if template is None:
    81→            raise ConfigurationError(f"Message template not found: {template_name}")
    82→        return template
    83→
    84→    def list_templates(self) -> list:
    85→        return list(self._message_templates.keys())
    86→
    87→
    88→_config_instance = None
    89→
    90→
    91→def get_config() -> Config:
    92→    global _config_instance
    93→    if _config_instance is None:
    94→        _config_instance = Config()
    95→    return _config_instance
    96→
    97→
    98→def reset_config() -> None:
    99→    """Reset global config instance (for testing)."""
   100→    global _config_instance
   101→    _config_instance = None

1→"""Custom exceptions for Boss Assistant."""
     2→
     3→
     4→class BossAssistantError(Exception):
     5→    """Base exception for all Boss Assistant errors."""
     6→    pass
     7→
     8→
     9→class BrowserError(BossAssistantError):
    10→    """Raised when browser automation fails."""
    11→    pass
    12→
    13→
    14→class SessionExpiredError(BossAssistantError):
    15→    """Raised when Boss直聘 session has expired."""
    16→    pass
    17→
    18→
    19→class RateLimitExceededError(BossAssistantError):
    20→    """Raised when rate limit is exceeded."""
    21→
    22→    def __init__(self, action_type: str, wait_time: int, message: str = None):
    23→        self.action_type = action_type
    24→        self.wait_time = wait_time
    25→        if message is None:
    26→            message = f"Rate limit exceeded for {action_type}. Wait {wait_time} seconds."
    27→        super().__init__(message)
    28→
    29→
    30→class SelectorNotFoundError(BossAssistantError):
    31→    """Raised when page selector is not found."""
    32→    pass
    33→
    34→
    35→class ExtractionError(BossAssistantError):
    36→    """Raised when data extraction fails."""
    37→    pass
    38→
    39→
    40→class ConfigurationError(BossAssistantError):
    41→    """Raised when configuration is invalid."""
    42→    pass
    43→
    44→
    45→class ValidationError(BossAssistantError):
    46→    """Raised when input validation fails."""
    47→    pass
    48→
    49→
    50→class PermanentFailureError(BossAssistantError):
    51→    """Raised when a send failure is deterministic and should not be retried.
    52→
    53→    Examples: no profile URL, quota exhausted, candidate already messaged.
    54→    """
    55→    pass

Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me review the plan and create tasks for the implementation.

The plan has 4 phases:
1. Phase 1: Basic framework - app.py, ChromeProcessManager, base templates, dashboard, status
2. Phase 2: Core operations - auth, search, candidates, export, templates routes + module modifications
3. Phase 3: Pipeline + SSE - TaskManager, pipeline modifications, SSE endpoint
4. Phase 4: Polish - Settings, error handling, run_web.py, CLAUDE.md

Let me create tasks and start implementing. This is a large project so I'll batch them.


I'll break this down into concrete tasks: first setting up the directory structure and dependencies, then building the ChromeProcessManager and FastAPI app with its lifespan hooks, creating the base templates and static assets, implementing the dashboard and status routes, updating BrowserManager with a reset method, and finally modifying the database layer to use NullPool and add the PipelineRun model. Phase 2 will follow with the auth routes and other core operations. I'm going to organize this by the phases already defined in the plan rather than creating tasks separately—I'll just execute batch by batch starting with the first three items from Phase 1.

@classmethod
    async def reset_instance(cls) -> None:
        """Reset the singleton instance, closing any existing connection.

        Used by Web service when Chrome process is restarted and a fresh
        connection is needed.
        """
        with cls._init_lock:
            if cls._instance is not None:
                try:
                    await cls._instance._close_internal()
                except Exception:
                    pass
                cls._instance = None
                cls._lock = None
                cls._lock_loop = None

        global _browser_manager
        _browser_manager = None

    def set_cdp_endpoint(self, endpoint: str) -> None:
        """Override CDP endpoint (e.g. from CLI --cdp flag)."""
        self._cdp_endpoint = endpoint

@classmethod
    async def reset_instance(cls) -> None:
        """Reset the singleton instance, closing any existing connection.

        Used by Web service when Chrome process is restarted and a fresh
        connection is needed.
        """
        with cls._init_lock:
            if cls._instance is not None:
                try:
                    await cls._instance._close_internal()
                except Exception:
                    pass
                cls._instance = None
                cls._lock = None
                cls._lock_loop = None

        global _browser_manager
        _browser_manager = None

    def set_cdp_endpoint(self, endpoint: str) -> None:

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""Browser manager for Playwright automation.

Provides a singleton browser manager that connects to an existing Chrome
instance via CDP (Chrome DevTools Protocol). Supports multi-tab management
for workflows that open new tabs.
"""

import asyncio
import threading
from pathlib import Path
from typing import List, Optional
from datetime import datetime

from patchright.async_api import async_playwright, Browser, BrowserContext, Page, Playwright
from loguru import logger

from src.utils.config import get_config, Config
from src.core.session import SessionManager, get_session_manager
from src.exceptions.errors import BrowserError


class BrowserManager:
    """
    Singleton browser manager for Boss直聘 automation.

    Connects to a user-managed Chrome instance via CDP endpoint.
    The user starts Chrome with --remote-debugging-port=9222 and logs in
    manually. This manager connects/disconnects without closing Chrome.
    """

    _instance: Optional['BrowserManager'] = None
    _lock: Optional[asyncio.Lock] = None
    _lock_loop = None
    _init_lock: threading.Lock = threading.Lock()

    def __new__(cls, **kwargs):
        with cls._init_lock:
            if cls._instance is None:
                cls._instance = super().__new__(cls)
                cls._instance._initialized = False
            return cls._instance

    def __init__(
        self,
        config: Optional[Config] = None,
        session_manager: Optional[SessionManager] = None,
    ):
        if self._initialized:
            return

        self._playwright: Optional[Playwright] = None
        self._browser: Optional[Browser] = None
        self._context: Optional[BrowserContext] = None
        self._page: Optional[Page] = None
        self._primary_page: Optional[Page] = None
        self._opened_tabs: List[Page] = []
        self._session_manager: SessionManager = session_manager or get_session_manager()
        self._config: Config = config or get_config()
        self._cdp_endpoint: Optional[str] = None
        self._owned_page: bool = False
        self._initialized = True

    @property
    def is_running(self) -> bool:
        """Check if browser is currently connected."""
        return self._browser is not None and self._browser.is_connected()

    @property
    def page(self) -> Optional[Page]:
        """Get current page instance."""
        return self._page

    @property
    def context(self) -> Optional[BrowserContext]:
        """Get current browser context."""
        return self._context

    @classmethod
    def _ensure_lock(cls):
        """Lazily create the asyncio.Lock inside a running event loop.

        Always recreates the lock when called from a different event loop
        than the one that created it. Python 3.10+ removed the _loop
        attribute from asyncio primitives, so we track the loop ourselves.
        """
        try:
            loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
        except RuntimeError:
            loop = None
        if cls._lock is None or (loop and getattr(cls, '_lock_loop', None) is not loop):
            cls._lock = asyncio.Lock()
            cls._lock_loop = loop

    def set_cdp_endpoint(self, endpoint: str) -> None:
        """Override CDP endpoint (e.g. from CLI --cdp flag)."""
        self._cdp_endpoint = endpoint

    async def start(self) -> Page:
        """
        Connect to Chrome via CDP and return a page.

        Returns:
            Configured Playwright page instance

        Raises:
            BrowserError: If connection fails
        """
        self._ensure_lock()
        async with self._lock:
            if self.is_running:
                logger.debug("Browser already connected, returning existing page")
                return self._page

            try:
                endpoint = self._cdp_endpoint or self._config.get(
                    'browser.cdp_endpoint', 'http://localhost:9222'
                )
                timeout = self._config.get('browser.timeout', 30000)

                logger.info(f"Connecting to Chrome via CDP: {endpoint}")

                self._playwright = await async_playwright().start()
                self._browser = await self._playwright.chromium.connect_over_cdp(endpoint)

                contexts = self._browser.contexts
                if contexts:
                    self._context = contexts[0]
                else:
                    logger.warning("No existing context found, creating new one")
                    self._context = await self._browser.new_context()

                self._context.set_default_timeout(timeout)

                pages = self._context.pages
                if pages:
                    self._page = pages[0]
                    self._owned_page = False
                    logger.info(f"Reusing existing tab: {self._page.url}")
                else:
                    self._page = await self._context.new_page()
                    self._owned_page = True
                    logger.info("Created new tab")

                self._primary_page = self._page
                self._opened_tabs = []

                logger.info("Connected to Chrome via CDP successfully")
                return self._page

            except Exception as e:
                logger.error(f"Failed to connect to Chrome via CDP: {e}")
                await self._close_internal()
                raise BrowserError(
                    f"Failed to connect to Chrome via CDP at {endpoint}. "
                    f"Make sure Chrome is running with --remote-debugging-port=9222. "
                    f"Error: {e}"
                )

    async def close(self) -> None:
        """Disconnect from Chrome via CDP. Does NOT close the user's Chrome."""
        self._ensure_lock()
        async with self._lock:
            await self._close_internal()

    async def _close_internal(self) -> None:
        """Actual cleanup logic."""
        try:
            for tab in self._opened_tabs:
                try:
                    if not tab.is_closed():
                        await tab.close()
                except Exception as e:
                    logger.debug(f"Error closing tab during cleanup: {e}")
            self._opened_tabs = []

            if self._page and self._owned_page:
                try:
                    await self._page.close()
                except Exception as e:
                    logger.debug(f"Error closing page during cleanup: {e}")
            self._page = None
            self._primary_page = None
            self._owned_page = False

            self._context = None

            if self._browser:
                try:
                    await self._browser.close()
                except Exception as e:
                    logger.error(f"Error closing browser connection: {e}")
                finally:
                    self._browser = None

            if self._playwright:
                try:
                    await self._playwright.stop()
                except Exception as e:
                    logger.error(f"Error stopping Playwright: {e}")
                finally:
                    self._playwright = None

            logger.info("Disconnected from Chrome (browser stays open)")

        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Error disconnecting from Chrome: {e}")

    async def wait_for_new_tab(self, timeout: int = 10000) -> Page:
        """Wait for a click-triggered new tab, apply stealth, and switch to it."""
        if not self._context:
            raise BrowserError("No active context for wait_for_new_tab")

        try:
            new_page = await self._context.wait_for_event(
                "page", timeout=timeout
            )
            await new_page.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded")
            self._opened_tabs.append(new_page)
            self._page = new_page
            logger.info(f"New tab captured: {new_page.url}")
            return new_page
        except Exception as e:
            raise BrowserError(f"No new tab opened within {timeout}ms: {e}")

    def switch_to_primary_tab(self) -> Page:
        """Switch back to the original (primary) tab."""
        if not self._primary_page or self._primary_page.is_closed():
            raise BrowserError("Primary tab is not available")

        self._page = self._primary_page
        logger.debug(f"Switched to primary tab: {self._page.url}")
        return self._page

    async def close_current_tab(self) -> Page:
        """Close the current active tab and switch to the previous one."""
        if not self._page:
            raise BrowserError("No active page to close")

        if self._page == self._primary_page:
            raise BrowserError("Cannot close the primary tab")

        closing = self._page
        try:
            if closing in self._opened_tabs:
                self._opened_tabs.remove(closing)
            await closing.close()
        except Exception as e:
            logger.warning(f"Error closing tab: {e}")

        if self._context and self._context.pages:
            self._page = self._context.pages[-1]
        else:
            self._page = self._primary_page

        logger.debug(f"Closed tab, switched to: {self._page.url if self._page else 'none'}")
        return self._page

    async def save_session(self) -> None:
        """Save current session to disk (backup of cookies)."""
        if self._context:
            await self._session_manager.save(self._context)
        else:
            logger.warning("No active context to save session from")

    async def screenshot(self, name: str = None) -> str:
        """Take a screenshot of current page."""
        if not self._page:
            raise BrowserError("No active page for screenshot")

        screenshot_dir = Path(self._config.get('data.screenshot_dir', 'data/screenshots'))
        screenshot_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

        if name is None:
            name = datetime.now().strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')

        filepath = screenshot_dir / f"{name}.png"

        await self._page.screenshot(path=str(filepath), full_page=True)
        logger.info(f"Screenshot saved: {filepath}")

        return str(filepath)


# Global browser manager instance
_browser_manager: Optional[BrowserManager] = None


def get_browser_manager() -> BrowserManager:
    """Get global browser manager instance."""
    global _browser_manager
    if _browser_manager is None:
        _browser_manager = BrowserManager()
    return _browser_manager

"""Database models and setup using SQLAlchemy."""

from contextlib import contextmanager
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional

from sqlalchemy import (
    Column, Integer, String, Text, DateTime, JSON,
    ForeignKey, create_engine, Index, CheckConstraint
)
from sqlalchemy.orm import declarative_base, sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.pool import StaticPool
from loguru import logger

from src.utils.config import get_config

Base = declarative_base()


def _utcnow():
    """Return timezone-aware UTC now."""
    return datetime.now(timezone.utc)


class Candidate(Base):
    """Candidate model - stores candidate profile information."""

    __tablename__ = 'candidates'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    boss_id = Column(String(50), unique=True, nullable=False, index=True)
    name = Column(String(100))
    title = Column(String(200))
    company = Column(String(200))
    location = Column(String(100))
    salary_expect = Column(String(100))      # Boss直聘: expected salary
    work_years = Column(String(50))           # Boss直聘: work years
    education_level = Column(String(50))      # Boss直聘: education level
    profile_url = Column(Text)

    # JSON fields for structured data
    basic_info = Column(JSON)
    education = Column(JSON)
    work_experience = Column(JSON)
    skills = Column(JSON)

    # Metadata
    first_seen = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow)
    last_updated = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow, onupdate=_utcnow)
    source_search = Column(Text)

    # Relationships
    messages = relationship("Message", back_populates="candidate")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Candidate(boss_id='{self.boss_id}', name='{self.name}', company='{self.company}')>"


class Message(Base):
    """Message model - stores sent messages to candidates."""

    __tablename__ = 'messages'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    candidate_boss_id = Column(String(50), ForeignKey('candidates.boss_id'), index=True)
    template_name = Column(String(100))
    content = Column(Text, nullable=False)
    status = Column(String(20), CheckConstraint("status IN ('sent', 'failed', 'cancelled')"))
    sent_at = Column(DateTime)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow)

    # Relationships
    candidate = relationship("Candidate", back_populates="messages")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Message(id={self.id}, candidate_id='{self.candidate_boss_id}', status='{self.status}')>"


class SearchHistory(Base):
    """Search history model - tracks all searches performed."""

    __tablename__ = 'search_history'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    criteria = Column(JSON)
    results_count = Column(Integer, default=0)
    executed_at = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow, index=True)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<SearchHistory(id={self.id}, results={self.results_count}, date='{self.executed_at}')>"


class ActionLog(Base):
    """Action log model - tracks all actions for rate limiting."""

    __tablename__ = 'action_logs'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    action_type = Column(String(50), nullable=False)
    timestamp = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow, nullable=False)
    details = Column(JSON)

    __table_args__ = (
        Index('idx_action_logs_type_time', 'action_type', 'timestamp'),
    )

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<ActionLog(id={self.id}, type='{self.action_type}', time='{self.timestamp}')>"


class DatabaseManager:
    """Database manager for initialization and session management."""

    def __init__(self, db_path: Optional[str] = None):
        if db_path is None:
            config = get_config()
            db_path = config.get('data.database_path', 'data/boss.db')

        db_file = Path(db_path)
        db_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

        self.db_path = db_path
        # StaticPool uses a single connection — safe for single-threaded CLI usage.
        # If concurrent async tasks are introduced, switch to NullPool or QueuePool.
        self.engine = create_engine(
            f'sqlite:///{db_path}',
            echo=False,
            poolclass=StaticPool,
            connect_args={"check_same_thread": False},
        )
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)

        self.init_db()
        logger.info(f"Database initialized at: {db_path}")

    def init_db(self):
        """Create all tables and run pending migrations."""
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
        logger.info("Database tables created successfully")

        from src.models.migrations import run_migrations
        run_migrations(self.engine)

    @contextmanager
    def session_scope(self):
        """Provide a transactional scope with auto commit/rollback/close."""
        session = self.Session()
        try:
            yield session
            session.commit()
        except Exception:
            session.rollback()
            raise
        finally:
            session.close()

    def drop_all(self):
        """Drop all tables (use with caution!)."""
        Base.metadata.drop_all(self.engine)
        logger.warning("All database tables dropped")


# Global database manager instance
_db_manager = None


def get_db_manager() -> DatabaseManager:
    """Get global database manager instance."""
    global _db_manager
    if _db_manager is None:
        _db_manager = DatabaseManager()
    return _db_manager


def reset_db_manager() -> None:
    """Reset global database manager (for testing)."""
    global _db_manager
    _db_manager = None

"""Chrome process lifecycle manager for Web service.

Manages starting, health-checking, and restarting the Chrome browser
process with CDP enabled. Used by the Web service lifespan to ensure
Chrome is always available for browser automation tasks.
"""

import asyncio
import shutil
import signal
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional

import httpx
from loguru import logger

from src.utils.config import get_config


class ChromeProcessManager:
    """Manages the Chrome browser process for the Web service.

    - Auto-starts Chrome in headed mode with --remote-debugging-port
    - Periodically health-checks via CDP HTTP endpoint
    - Auto-restarts Chrome if it crashes
    - Gracefully terminates Chrome on shutdown
    """

    def __init__(self):
        self._config = get_config()
        self._process: Optional[subprocess.Popen] = None
        self._health_task: Optional[asyncio.Task] = None
        self._running = False

    @property
    def debug_port(self) -> int:
        return self._config.get('web.chrome_debug_port', 9222)

    @property
    def user_data_dir(self) -> str:
        return self._config.get('web.chrome_user_data_dir', 'data/.chrome-profile')

    @property
    def cdp_url(self) -> str:
        return f'http://127.0.0.1:{self.debug_port}'

    @property
    def is_chrome_running(self) -> bool:
        return self._process is not None and self._process.poll() is None

    def _find_chrome_binary(self) -> str:
        """Find Chrome binary on the current platform."""
        if sys.platform == 'darwin':
            candidates = [
                '/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome',
                '/Applications/Chromium.app/Contents/MacOS/Chromium',
            ]
        elif sys.platform == 'win32':
            candidates = [
                r'C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe',
                r'C:\Program Files (x86)\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe',
            ]
        else:
            candidates = [
                'google-chrome',
                'google-chrome-stable',
                'chromium',
                'chromium-browser',
            ]

        for candidate in candidates:
            if Path(candidate).exists() or shutil.which(candidate):
                return candidate

        raise RuntimeError(
            'Chrome binary not found. Install Google Chrome or set the path manually.'
        )

    async def _check_cdp_health(self) -> bool:
        """Check if Chrome CDP endpoint is responsive."""
        try:
            async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
                resp = await client.get(
                    f'{self.cdp_url}/json/version',
                    timeout=5.0,
                )
                return resp.status_code == 200
        except Exception:
            return False

    def _start_chrome_process(self) -> None:
        """Start Chrome with CDP enabled."""
        chrome_binary = self._find_chrome_binary()
        user_data = Path(self.user_data_dir).resolve()
        user_data.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

        args = [
            chrome_binary,
            f'--remote-debugging-port={self.debug_port}',
            f'--user-data-dir={user_data}',
            '--no-first-run',
            '--no-default-browser-check',
            '--disable-background-timer-throttling',
            '--disable-backgrounding-occluded-windows',
            '--disable-renderer-backgrounding',
        ]

        logger.info(f'Starting Chrome: {" ".join(args[:3])}...')
        self._process = subprocess.Popen(
            args,
            stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL,
            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,
        )
        logger.info(f'Chrome started with PID {self._process.pid}')

    async def start(self) -> None:
        """Start Chrome and begin health monitoring.

        If Chrome is already running on the debug port (e.g. user started
        it manually), we skip launching a new process and just monitor.
        """
        self._running = True

        # Check if Chrome is already running on the port
        if await self._check_cdp_health():
            logger.info(f'Chrome already running on port {self.debug_port}, skipping launch')
        elif self._config.get('web.chrome_auto_launch', True):
            self._start_chrome_process()
            # Wait for Chrome to be ready
            for _ in range(30):
                if await self._check_cdp_health():
                    logger.info('Chrome CDP endpoint is ready')
                    break
                await asyncio.sleep(1)
            else:
                logger.error('Chrome failed to start within 30 seconds')
        else:
            logger.warning(
                'Chrome auto-launch disabled and no Chrome detected on '
                f'port {self.debug_port}'
            )

        # Start health check loop
        self._health_task = asyncio.create_task(self._health_loop())

    async def _health_loop(self) -> None:
        """Periodically check Chrome health and restart if needed."""
        while self._running:
            await asyncio.sleep(10)
            if not self._running:
                break

            healthy = await self._check_cdp_health()
            if not healthy:
                logger.warning('Chrome health check failed')
                if self._config.get('web.chrome_auto_launch', True):
                    await self._restart_chrome()

    async def _restart_chrome(self) -> None:
        """Restart Chrome process and reset BrowserManager connection."""
        logger.info('Restarting Chrome...')

        # Kill existing process if any
        self._kill_process()

        await asyncio.sleep(2)

        # Start fresh
        self._start_chrome_process()

        # Wait for ready
        for _ in range(30):
            if await self._check_cdp_health():
                logger.info('Chrome restarted successfully')
                break
            await asyncio.sleep(1)
        else:
            logger.error('Chrome restart failed')
            return

        # Reset BrowserManager singleton so it reconnects
        from src.core.browser import BrowserManager
        await BrowserManager.reset_instance()

    def _kill_process(self) -> None:
        """Terminate the Chrome process."""
        if self._process is None:
            return

        try:
            self._process.send_signal(signal.SIGTERM)
            try:
                self._process.wait(timeout=5)
            except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
                self._process.kill()
                self._process.wait(timeout=5)
            logger.info('Chrome process terminated')
        except Exception as e:
            logger.warning(f'Error terminating Chrome: {e}')
        finally:
            self._process = None

    async def stop(self) -> None:
        """Stop health monitoring and terminate Chrome."""
        self._running = False

        if self._health_task and not self._health_task.done():
            self._health_task.cancel()
            try:
                await self._health_task
            except asyncio.CancelledError:
                pass

        self._kill_process()
        logger.info('ChromeProcessManager stopped')

"""FastAPI application factory for Boss Assistant Web service."""

from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
from pathlib import Path

from fastapi import FastAPI, Request
from fastapi.staticfiles import StaticFiles
from fastapi.templating import Jinja2Templates
from loguru import logger

from src.web.services.chrome_manager import ChromeProcessManager
from src.web.services.task_manager import TaskManager

WEB_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
TEMPLATES_DIR = WEB_DIR / "templates"
STATIC_DIR = WEB_DIR / "static"

# Shared instances
chrome_manager = ChromeProcessManager()
task_manager = TaskManager()
templates = Jinja2Templates(directory=str(TEMPLATES_DIR))


@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    """Application lifespan: start/stop Chrome and DB."""
    logger.info("Starting Boss Assistant Web service...")

    # Initialize database
    from src.models.database import get_db_manager
    get_db_manager()

    # Start Chrome
    await chrome_manager.start()

    yield

    # Shutdown
    logger.info("Shutting down Boss Assistant Web service...")
    await task_manager.cancel_all()
    await chrome_manager.stop()

    # Close browser connection
    from src.core.browser import BrowserManager
    await BrowserManager.reset_instance()

    logger.info("Shutdown complete")


def create_app() -> FastAPI:
    """Create and configure the FastAPI application."""
    app = FastAPI(
        title="Boss Assistant",
        description="Boss直聘半自动招聘助手",
        lifespan=lifespan,
    )

    # Mount static files
    app.mount("/static", StaticFiles(directory=str(STATIC_DIR)), name="static")

    # Register routes
    from src.web.routes.dashboard import router as dashboard_router
    from src.web.routes.auth import router as auth_router
    from src.web.routes.search import router as search_router
    from src.web.routes.candidates import router as candidates_router
    from src.web.routes.messages import router as messages_router
    from src.web.routes.pipeline import router as pipeline_router
    from src.web.routes.export import router as export_router
    from src.web.routes.templates import router as templates_router
    from src.web.routes.settings import router as settings_router
    from src.web.routes.status import router as status_router

    app.include_router(dashboard_router)
    app.include_router(auth_router, prefix="/auth", tags=["auth"])
    app.include_router(search_router, prefix="/search", tags=["search"])
    app.include_router(candidates_router, prefix="/candidates", tags=["candidates"])
    app.include_router(messages_router, prefix="/messages", tags=["messages"])
    app.include_router(pipeline_router, prefix="/pipeline", tags=["pipeline"])
    app.include_router(export_router, prefix="/export", tags=["export"])
    app.include_router(templates_router, prefix="/templates", tags=["templates"])
    app.include_router(settings_router, prefix="/settings", tags=["settings"])
    app.include_router(status_router, prefix="/status", tags=["status"])

    return app
